title: Classify each license as permissive, weak-copyleft, or strong-copyleft
description: Sort dependencies by the reciprocity strength of their license, because that class drives what you must publish back.
body: Use when assessing whether a dependency's obligations are compatible with how you ship. Do not treat all OSI-approved licenses as interchangeable. Actions: classify each license as permissive (MIT, BSD, Apache-2.0), weak/file-level copyleft (LGPL, MPL-2.0, EPL), or strong/network copyleft (GPL-2.0, GPL-3.0, AGPL-3.0); record which class each component lands in and how it is linked. Validation signals include a classification column in the inventory and a test asserting no strong-copyleft component appears in a closed-source linked artifact. Risk boundary: AGPL in a SaaS backend or GPL in a statically linked proprietary binary triggers obligations that must go to a human before release.
